tasks.title: Implement Centralized Authentication and RBAC for Microservices
tasks.description: Address the missing explicit authentication and Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) across microservices. This involves implementing an API Gateway or service mesh to enforce mTLS for inter-service communication and JWT/RBAC validation for all administrative and sensitive financial endpoints.
subtasks: Research and select an API Gateway or service mesh solution (e.g., Istio, Envoy Gateway).
Configure mTLS for inter-service communication between Catalog, RMS, Finance, Vetting, and AI Agent services.
Implement JWT validation middleware for all API endpoints, especially administrative and financial ones.
Define and implement RBAC policies for sensitive operations in Catalog (admin endpoints), Finance, and RMS services.
Integrate RBAC checks into the application logic of affected services.
Conduct penetration testing to verify authentication and authorization controls.
acceptance_criteria: All inter-service communication is enforced via mTLS.
Access to `/api/v1/catalog/products` (POST/PATCH), all Finance service endpoints, and sensitive RMS endpoints requires a valid JWT.
RBAC policies correctly restrict unauthorized users from performing administrative or financial operations.
Penetration test confirms no unauthorized access to sensitive endpoints.

tasks.title: Enhance Input Validation and Sanitization for AI Agent Backend APIs
tasks.description: Mitigate prompt injection risks by implementing rigorous input validation and sanitization on all backend API endpoints that the AI Agent interacts with. The AI Agent should be treated as an untrusted client, and all incoming parameters must be strictly validated against expected formats and types.
subtasks: Identify all API endpoints in Catalog, RMS, Finance, and Vetting services that are exposed to the AI Agent via MCP tools.
Implement strict schema validation (e.g., JSON schema, OpenAPI validation) for all incoming request bodies and query parameters on identified endpoints.
Apply context-aware sanitization for all string inputs to prevent injection attacks (e.g., SQL injection, command injection, XSS if applicable).
Ensure numerical and boolean parameters are strictly type-checked.
Add logging for validation failures to detect potential attack attempts.
Conduct security testing (e.g., fuzzing, negative testing) against these endpoints.
acceptance_criteria: All API endpoints called by the AI Agent (via MCP tools) have explicit input validation.
Invalid or malicious inputs are rejected with appropriate error messages (e.g., 400 Bad Request).
No prompt injection vulnerabilities are discoverable through testing.
Security scans (e.g., OWASP ZAP) confirm robust input validation.

tasks.title: Secure Database Credentials and Eliminate Exposure in ConfigMaps
tasks.description: Address the potential exposure of database credentials by moving them from ConfigMaps to Kubernetes Secrets. Database connection strings should be constructed within the application code using credentials injected as environment variables from Secrets.
subtasks: Modify `sigma1-infra-endpoints` ConfigMap creation (subtask 1005) to *exclude* sensitive credentials.
Create new Kubernetes Secrets for PostgreSQL and Redis/Valkey credentials (e.g., `sigma1-postgres-credentials`, `sigma1-valkey-credentials`) in the `databases` namespace.
Update deployment manifests for Catalog (Task 2), RMS (Task 3), Finance (Task 4), and Vetting (Task 5) services to inject these Secrets as environment variables.
Modify application code in affected services to construct database connection strings using the injected environment variables.
Verify that no sensitive credentials remain in any ConfigMaps.
acceptance_criteria: `sigma1-infra-endpoints` ConfigMap does not contain any sensitive credentials.
PostgreSQL and Redis/Valkey credentials are stored exclusively in Kubernetes Secrets.
All services successfully connect to their respective databases using credentials from Secrets.
`kubectl get configmap -n sigma1 sigma1-infra-endpoints -o yaml` shows no sensitive data.
`kubectl get secret -n databases` shows the new credential secrets.

tasks.title: Implement Encryption at Rest and Field-Level Encryption for Sensitive Data
tasks.description: Ensure sensitive business data handled by Finance and Vetting services is protected through encryption. This involves enabling encryption at rest for PostgreSQL databases and implementing field-level encryption for highly sensitive PII/financial data within the application layer.
subtasks: Update the PostgreSQL provisioning (subtask 1002) to ensure encryption at rest is enabled for the `sigma1-postgres` cluster. This might involve cloud provider settings or specific operator configurations.
Identify highly sensitive fields in Finance (e.g., payment details, specific report data) and Vetting (e.g., credit signals, specific business verification data) services.
Select an appropriate encryption library/method for field-level encryption in Rust (Finance, Vetting) and Go (RMS, if applicable for future sensitive data).
Implement field-level encryption for identified sensitive data fields in the Finance and Vetting services before storage and decryption upon retrieval.
Manage encryption keys securely (e.g., using Kubernetes Secrets or a KMS).
Conduct data access tests to confirm data is encrypted at rest and field-level encryption/decryption works correctly.
acceptance_criteria: PostgreSQL database for `sigma1-postgres` is configured with encryption at rest.
Sensitive fields in Finance and Vetting services are stored in an encrypted format in the database.
Applications can correctly encrypt and decrypt field-level data without errors.
Review of database contents confirms sensitive data is not stored in plain text.
